text: Fill in the blanks to test your knowledge of vitamins and nutrition.
questions: Fat-soluble vitamins are absorbed with dietary fat via *micelles* and require *bile/bile salts* for absorption.
Vitamin A deficiency causes *night blindness/nyctalopia* and foamy white patches on the conjunctiva called *Bitot's/Bitot* spots.
The three active forms of Vitamin A are *retinol*, *retinal*, and *retinoic acid*.
Vitamin D deficiency affects *70/seventy* to 90 percent of Indians and causes *rickets* in children and *osteomalacia* in adults.
Water-soluble vitamins are excreted in *urine* and have *low/minimal* toxicity risk, except *B12* which is stored in the liver for 3-5 years.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the fat-soluble and water-soluble vitamin properties and deficiency diseases.
Good progress! Revisit the active forms of vitamins and their clinical correlates.
Excellent! You have a strong understanding of vitamins and nutrition.
